instructions: Strictly base all content on the provided inputs and knowledge base.
Do not invent or hallucinate facts, figures, dates, precedents, partnerships, or staff details.
If information is missing, explicitly state that it is not available in the context.
All information is to be collected on a non-committal basis.
Focus on projects suitable for predictable earmarked funding.

The Mission of Japan will screen candidate projects based on diplomatic significance, benefits to the Japanese economy or companies, economic security importance, and past project management performance.
Because Japan follows a single-year budgeting system, request only the portion required for FY2027; for multi-year projects, provide a fiscal-year breakdown for subsequent years.
All UNHCR project proposals must be reviewed and cleared by the Donor Relations and Resource Mobilization Service (DRRM) at UNHCR Headquarters.
Projects must commence no later than March 2028.
Project descriptions should be as specific as possible and include quantitative data where available.
Performance targets should be quantitative and verifiable post-implementation through the PDCA cycle.
Provide as much budget detail as possible.
Do not include the JSB-specific elements of urgency, obligatoriness, unpredictability, or non-substitutability.
Use a flat section structure only: do not use subsections, items, or suggested bullet lists. Any required category labels or prompts must be written directly inside the section instructions.
When referencing donor, use donor name and never donor_id.
When referencing outcomes, use outcome name and never outcome_id.
When referencing field context, use field_context name and never field_context_id.

sections.instructions: Provide a compact opening snapshot that captures the front-matter information.

columns.name: Field Context
columns.instructions: Field context where the project will be implemented.

rows.instructions: Country where the project will be implemented.

sections.instructions: Summarize the crisis or operational context, the latest humanitarian needs, the target population, and the core value proposition of the project. State the project objective clearly within the narrative, explain why the response is needed now, and include the implementation period in concise form. State the objective of the proposed project clearly and concisely. Summarize the crisis or operational context, the latest humanitarian needs, the target population, and the core value proposition of the project. State the project objective clearly within the narrative, explain why the response is needed now, and include the implementation period in concise form. Open with quantified context, highlight the most vulnerable groups, and end with a clear statement of what the project will deliver. Indicate in narrative form whether the project falls under any of the following categories, and briefly justify each category that applies: food assistance, health, Green Transformation (GX), Digital Transformation (DX), Women Peace and Security (WPS), regional integration or connectivity, youth, public-private partnership (PPP), rule of law, and Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). For SDGs, list all applicable goal numbers. If none apply or information is missing, state this explicitly.

Describe the proposed activities as specifically as possible, including quantitative data where available. Address the following within one concise text section, where applicable and supported by the context: diplomatic significance or strategic importance of the project; partnerships or collaboration with Japanese companies or NGOs; coordination or synergy with projects implemented directly by MOFA or through JICA; whether the project is TICAD-related; whether the project promotes the Humanitarian-Development Nexus; and how the project includes verifiable performance targets under the PDCA cycle. If any element is not available in the context, state this explicitly.

Describe the overall impact, the expected outcomes, and the main outputs or activity streams in a concise but structured narrative. Embed performance targets directly in the text by specifying quantitative, verifiable results such as the number of beneficiaries, services delivered, institutions supported, or outputs produced. Link each outcome to a short explanation of the intervention logic, the population reached, and at least one concrete indicator or target that can be verified after implementation through the PDCA cycle. If the project aligns with any of Japan's 17 strategic fields, identify all applicable fields in the narrative and briefly explain the relevance. The strategic fields are AI or semiconductors, digital or cybersecurity, ICT, quantum, defense industry, aerospace, maritime, shipbuilding, materials including critical minerals, synthetic biology or bio, drug discovery or advanced medical care, resource and energy security or GX, fusion energy, foodtech, disaster prevention or national resilience, port logistics, and content. If no alignment is available in the context, state this explicitly.

xplain why the project is strategically relevant and why support from Japan adds value. Within one coherent section, address the response gap being filled, the humanitarian and protection rationale, and the benefits to Japan, including any supported relevance to Japanese diplomatic priorities, human security, the Humanitarian-Development-Peace Nexus, Women, Peace and Security, benefits to Japanese companies or the Japanese economy, and economic security considerations. If the project contributes to support for Japanese personnel, include the staff member name, current title or rank, duty station, type of support such as securing new posts, promotions, or regular recruitment of JPOs, and the expected impact. If this is not applicable or not available in the context, state this explicitly.
State the planned implementation period and confirm that the project will commence no later than March 2028.
Provide as much detail as possible. For multi-year projects, show only the FY2027 requested amount and include the indicative breakdown for subsequent fiscal years.

columns.name: Budget Category
columns.instructions: Major cost category, for example staffing, supplies, cash assistance, logistics, monitoring, or support costs.

columns.name: Subsequent Fiscal Years Breakdown
columns.instructions: If applicable, provide indicative cost breakdown for each subsequent fiscal year.

rows.instructions: Estimated support or indirect costs. This should not be more than 6.6% of the total budget.
